title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs upgrade, connect, tunnels, websocket boundaries, and safe transitions from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Transition Rejection Recovery
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 5190 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.

Inspect the Transition Rejection Recovery trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Transition Rejection Recovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for continue http/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Transition Rejection Recovery, keep Transition Rejection Recovery connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
